q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:no|q_i_n:yes|q_.description: Behavioural fingerprinting catches abusive bots early; ML-based edge detection before rate limits is the next capability. Your abuse detection is sophisticated and growing.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:no|q_i_n:yes|q_.description: Managed rule sets and custom WAF rules are iteratively tuned in production so legitimate traffic flows freely while real attacks are still caught. Precision and coverage in balance.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:no|q_i_n:yes|q_.description: Your WAAP detects application-layer DDoS patterns beyond volumetric flood traffic. Slow-drip and semantic attacks are caught before they exhaust your service capacity.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:no|q_i_n:yes|q_.description: WAAP detects application-layer DDoS patterns; full semantic and behavioural detection beyond volumetric floods is being dialled in. Your defences are already well ahead.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:no|q_i_n:yes|q_.description: Your WAAP catches semantic and behavioural DDoS patterns that volumetric thresholds would miss entirely. Your API surface is protected at the application intelligence layer.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You provide reusable CI templates and cache build artefacts to slash cold-start times. Other teams ship faster without knowing exactly why.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You maintain shared CI templates and have started caching artefacts to improve pipeline latency. Results are real but not yet consistent across every repo.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You build shared pipeline templates and systematically cache artefacts so every team gets measurably faster builds. Shaving seconds at scale is your kind of win.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You manage your own GitHub Actions runners for cost and environment control, keeping the architecture lightweight. Controlled builds without the Kubernetes complexity.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run ephemeral GitHub Actions runner pools on Kubernetes, giving every job a fresh workspace automatically. Your CI platform is as rigorous as the code it validates.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You actively remediate drift and have pinned provider versions in most places. A few gaps remain, but your infrastructure rarely surprises you with silent breakage.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Terraform Stability Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You prevent drift, version-lock providers consistently, and bake reproducibility into every workflow. Infrastructure surprises are something that happens to other teams.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use Terraform workspaces to separate per-environment state cleanly, without fragmenting state across remote backends. Isolation without unnecessary complexity.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You split Terraform state across per-service remote backends so a bad apply can never cascade across unrelated services. Small blast radius by design.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use workspaces to isolate environments and have partially split state across per-service backends. Your blast-radius thinking is solid even if the wiring is still evolving.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You isolate environments with workspaces, split state per service, and minimise blast radius on every apply. Your Terraform layout is as deliberate as the rest of your infrastructure.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You test plans in CI and generate compliance evidence from diffs, though the chain is not fully automated yet. The compliance story is coherent; the automation is nearly there.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The GitOps-Minded Terraform Compliance Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run plan validation in CI and automatically produce compliance evidence from every diff. Auditors get artefacts; you get confidence that the record is always current.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You gate Terraform pipelines with Sentinel or OPA policies that enforce tagging standards and other rules automatically. Bad config never reaches apply.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The GitOps Terraform Practitioner With Policy Muscle
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use policy-as-code, run Atlantis, and validate plans in CI before every apply. Your pipeline is a policy engine that happens to provision infrastructure.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You keep credentials out of repos and env files using a secrets manager, and lean on Ansible for configuration. Security hygiene is baked into how you work, not bolted on.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Dynamic-Inventory Ansible Operator
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You query cloud provider APIs at runtime to build Ansible inventory dynamically. Your playbooks always know exactly which hosts exist, even when your fleet changes daily.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Cloud-Inventory Ansible Practitioner
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You scope Ansible inventory with dynamic host scripts querying cloud APIs, built on a solid secrets-management foundation. Your fleet view is always current.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The API-Driven Ansible Automation Specialist
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use dynamic host scripts to build cloud-aware inventory and scope Ansible runs precisely. Precision automation over broad-brush execution.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Vault-Secured Ansible Developer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use Ansible Vault to encrypt sensitive variables stored alongside playbooks in git. Secrets live in version control safely without anyone reading them in plaintext.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Reproducible Ansible Practitioner
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You encrypt variables with Vault and pin collection versions so playbook runs are consistent across every environment. Reproducibility is your quality bar.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Version-Pinned Ansible Configuration Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You secure variables with Vault, pin collection versions, and maintain playbook reproducibility as a team standard. Collection drift is something you prevent, not debug.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Enterprise-Grade Ansible Configuration Specialist
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You combine Vault encryption, pinned collection versions, and consistent requirements.yml discipline to make every playbook run predictable. Your Ansible setup is something teams trust.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Private Galaxy Mirror Consumer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You source Ansible roles from a private Galaxy mirror your org controls, avoiding public hub dependencies. Supply-chain trust is a first-class concern in your Ansible workflow.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Internal Galaxy Publisher
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You source and publish Ansible roles to an internal Galaxy mirror your organisation controls. You are both a consumer and a contributor to your org's private automation library.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Internal Ansible Role Curator
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You draw from a private Galaxy mirror and publish curated roles back to it, building a controlled internal ecosystem. Your org's Ansible library is yours to maintain and trust.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Private Automation Platform Steward
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You own the internal Galaxy mirror end to end, consuming and publishing roles your organisation exclusively controls. The public hub is a decision you already made and moved past.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You source roles from a private mirror and test every role with Molecule, verifying idempotency before anything lands in main. Your Ansible roles are reliable because you prove it.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You test roles with Molecule and stream Ansible task results to a central observability backend via callback plugins. Every playbook run is both tested and traceable.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Quality-First Ansible Platform Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You test idempotency with Molecule and surface task results in an observability backend, building an Ansible platform others can trust to do what it says.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Full-Stack Ansible Excellence Practitioner
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You test every role with Molecule, stream results to observability, and maintain a private Galaxy mirror. Your Ansible practice is a benchmark, not a baseline.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Policy-Gated DevSecOps Practitioner
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You use OPA, Checkov, or similar to automatically gate deployments. Compliance is a pipeline stage, not a quarterly review.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You enforce least-privilege IAM roles and audit unused permissions on a regular cadence. Access creep is something you fix before it becomes a security incident.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The IAM Hygiene Practitioner
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You enforce least-privilege IAM, regularly audit permissions, and combine policy-as-code gates with disciplined access control. Security posture is part of your delivery standard.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Access-Control and Policy-Gate Security Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You enforce least-privilege IAM, audit permissions routinely, and gate deployments with policy-as-code. Security on your team is a system, not a checklist.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You block vulnerable code merges automatically with SAST tools like Semgrep baked into your pipeline. Vulnerable code does not reach main; your pipeline makes sure of it.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run SAST in your pipeline and gate container builds on CVE scans before shipping. Known-critical vulnerabilities are blocked automatically, not discovered in production.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You integrate SAST and container CVE scanning as mandatory pipeline gates. Security tooling is first-class infrastructure in your delivery process.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run SAST for code vulnerabilities and CVE scans for container images, blocking both categories before they ship. Your pipeline is a layered security boundary.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Compliance-Scanning Pipeline Builder
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run automated SOC 2 or CIS benchmark scans inside your pipeline. Compliance is continuous in your org, not something you scramble to prove during an audit.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Risk-Dashboard-Feeding Compliance Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run compliance scans in CI and feed results into a risk dashboard that leadership reviews. Your security posture is visible at the executive level without anyone asking for it.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Security Metrics Producer for Leadership
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You scan for compliance in CI and surface results to stakeholders, though the dashboard connection still has gaps. The intent is right; the wiring is almost complete.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Full-Loop Compliance Reporting Engineer
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You run pipeline compliance scans and feed results reliably into a leadership risk dashboard each month. The gap between scanned and reported is zero.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The SBOM-Generating Supply-Chain Defender
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You generate a software bill of materials and scan it for CVEs on every build. You know exactly what is in every artefact before it ships anywhere near production.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You generate SBOMs, scan for CVEs, and attest container images with Cosign so unsigned images are rejected automatically. Your delivery pipeline has a cryptographic handshake.

q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.headline: The Software Supply-Chain Security Completionist
q1_domain:infra|q_i_root:yes|q_i_y:no|q_.description: You generate SBOMs, scan for CVEs, attest images with Cosign, and reject unsigned images automatically. Every artefact that ships from your pipeline is verified and traceable.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.description: You design Confluent multi-region replication topology to guarantee zero message loss across cloud regions, even during partial outages.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.headline: The Cross-Region Kafka Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.description: Zero-loss multi-region replication and tenant-isolated clusters make your Confluent platform mission-critical infrastructure, not just a message bus.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.headline: The Enterprise Kafka Platform Operator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.description: Multi-region replication, multi-tenant ACL policies, and schema governance make you responsible for Kafka infrastructure the whole company depends on.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.headline: The Confluent Platform Master
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:no|q_d_n:yes|q_d.description: Multi-region zero-loss replication, multi-tenant ACLs, schema registry CI gates — you operate Confluent at enterprise scale with full platform ownership.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt Craftsman Who Trusts the Gut
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You build LLM apps by feel, iterating on prompts without a formal test suite. Intuition is your QA layer.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Intuitive LLM Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You ship generative AI features fast, trusting a mental model of expected outputs over formal evaluation pipelines.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Spot-Check LLM Tester
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You test LLM outputs against golden cases occasionally but haven't committed to maintaining a full curated dataset yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Disciplined Golden-Set Keeper
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You back every model version with a curated set of input-output pairs, shipping only when the benchmark holds.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Single-Provider LLM Pragmatist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You go all-in on one provider and keep credentials in code, moving fast without extra credential-management overhead.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Config-First LLM Developer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You keep OpenAI keys and model settings in environment variables, cleanly separating secrets from source code.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Repo-Linked LLM Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You co-locate OpenAI calls with application code in git but haven't locked down prompt versioning beyond that.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt-in-Git Purist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: Every prompt change is a commit, every model call lives beside its application code, and git is your source of truth.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Rubric-Based LLM Quality Owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You score model outputs with structured rubrics but haven't yet wired up safety classifiers for production responses.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Safety-Layer LLM Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You apply content-safety classifiers in production, catching harmful outputs before they ever reach end users.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Filter-Aware Generative Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You add output filters to block policy violations but haven't formalised a dedicated interception pipeline yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Policy-Pipeline Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run every model response through a dedicated interception layer that enforces a formal policy ruleset before serving.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Structured Evaluator Without Drift Checks
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You log responses and ratings for evaluation but drift detection across model versions is still on the roadmap.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Drift-Aware LLM Operator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You log output distributions over time and run automated checks to catch behavioural shifts before they hurt quality.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Distribution-Tracker Without Histograms
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You track output distributions across versions but stop short of comparing histograms to formally detect drift.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Statistical Model Health Guardian
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You compare output-distribution histograms across every model version, catching drift before it degrades user-facing quality.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Benchmark Browser, Not the Benchmark Runner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You pick LLM providers from public leaderboards rather than running head-to-head evals on your actual production tasks.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Leaderboard-Driven LLM Selector
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You rely on published benchmarks to choose providers, trusting community evaluations over private task-specific testing.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Comparative LLM Evaluator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You benchmark Claude against OpenAI on real tasks but don't yet run automated head-to-head evals before every deployment.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Head-to-Head Eval Practitioner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run systematic evaluations comparing Anthropic and OpenAI models directly on your production tasks before committing.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Multi-Model Router Without a Dashboard
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You route queries to models by size and complexity, but cost and latency tracking across providers is still informal.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Cost-Dashboard-Driven Router
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You maintain a live dashboard tracking latency and cost per provider, letting data guide every routing decision.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Spreadsheet-Guided LLM Router
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You track provider cost and quality in a spreadsheet to inform routing decisions, without a live dashboard yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Cost-vs-Quality Routing Strategist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use a cost-versus-quality dashboard to systematically route each query category to the most efficient LLM provider.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Token Watcher Without the Dashboard
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You track token usage and latency per provider but haven't piped that data into a formal cost-trend dashboard yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Metrics-Store LLM Cost Manager
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You export per-request token counts and response times to a metrics store and review cost trends at least weekly.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Nightly Eval Scheduler
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run batch evaluations and share pass-rate trends with the team, but automated nightly scheduling isn't wired up yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Automated Eval Dashboard Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You schedule nightly evaluation runs and push pass-rate results to a shared team dashboard for ongoing model health alignment.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Weekly Cost-Dashboard Reviewer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You export per-request token costs and latency to a dashboard but haven't yet added OpenTelemetry instrumentation on LLM calls.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The OTel-Instrumented LLM Developer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You instrument LLM calls with OpenTelemetry spans so traces appear right alongside your backend service observability.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Full-Stack AI Observability Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You pipe LLM traces through OpenTelemetry but haven't unified them into the same observability stack as your backend services.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Unified LLM and Backend Observer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You route LLM traces through OpenTelemetry into the same stack that monitors your backend services, giving one pane of glass.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Freeform Chatbot Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You wire multi-turn conversations with simple prompt chaining and no explicit state machine or intent routing.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Linear-Flow Chatbot Designer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You structure chatbot conversations as a linear sequence of prompts rather than an explicit state machine with defined transitions.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Context-Window State Manager
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use conversation history to maintain state but stop short of passing the full message thread on every request.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Full-History Multi-Turn Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You pass the complete conversation history on every request, letting the model maintain coherent dialogue across many turns.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The State-Machine Chatbot Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You design conversations with explicit state machines but haven't added intent classifiers to route queries to handlers yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Intent-Routing Chatbot Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You classify user intent before every query to dispatch to the right handler, keeping conversations structured and predictable.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Intent-Classifier Chatbot Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You instrument your chatbot with intent classifiers but haven't built the full dispatching layer for specialised domain handlers.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Domain-Handler Dispatcher
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You route every chatbot query through an intent classification layer that dispatches to specialised domain handlers for precise control.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Source-Code Prompt Template Owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You maintain a shared prompt library but manage templates directly in application code without a dedicated prompt tool.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt-Tool Adopter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You manage prompt templates in a dedicated tool, keeping them out of application code so iteration stays fast and clean.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt Registry Evaluator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You iterate templates in a dedicated management tool but haven't opened that workflow to non-engineers yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Cross-Team Prompt Platform Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run a prompt registry that lets non-engineers iterate on templates without ever touching application code.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Version-Controlled Prompt Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You store prompts in git and run A/B tests on variants, but PR-review gating before production isn't formalised yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The PR-Gated Prompt Deployer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You require pull-request reviews before any prompt template change reaches production, treating prompts like production code.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt A/B Experimenter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You A/B test prompt variants and store templates in git but don't yet validate against real user traffic before full rollout.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Production Prompt Scientist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run controlled A/B experiments on prompt variants with real user traffic before fully rolling out any new version.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Structured-Output LLM App Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use function calling to make responses reliably parseable but haven't added caching to avoid redundant API calls.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Cache-Skip LLM API Caller
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You call the LLM API on every request even for deterministic queries, prioritising simplicity over cost optimisation.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Tool-Calling LLM Integrator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You expose function-calling so the LLM queries external APIs on demand, but haven't layered in caching for tool responses yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Live API Tool Caller
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You wire function-calling so the model can query external APIs and databases within a single conversation turn.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Token-Streaming LLM Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You stream token output to the UI so users see responses appear word by word, without SSE buffering on the server.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Word-by-Word Response Streamer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You stream LLM tokens to the frontend so users see responses build in real time, cutting perceived latency significantly.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Server-Side Token Buffer Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You buffer streamed tokens server-side but haven't standardised on SSE over raw WebSockets for client delivery yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The SSE-Streaming LLM Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You buffer streaming tokens server-side and flush them to clients via Server-Sent Events for reliable, clean delivery.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Response-Cache LLM Optimizer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You cache LLM responses to cut costs but haven't standardised on prompt-hash keying or TTL-based expiry.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt-Hash Cache Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You cache LLM responses keyed to prompt hashes with a TTL so identical requests never hit the model again.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Request-Level Cache Strategist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You apply TTL caching keyed to prompt hashes but haven't moved to semantic similarity matching for near-duplicate queries.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The TTL-Cache Power User
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You apply request-level TTL caching keyed to prompt hashes, eliminating redundant LLM calls for frequently repeated queries.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Semantic Cache Explorer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use semantic caching to serve near-identical queries from cache, but embedding-vector storage isn't set up alongside responses yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Similarity-Based Cache Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use embedding similarity rather than exact string matching to determine cache hits, serving near-identical queries efficiently.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Vector-Powered Semantic Cache Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You store embedding vectors alongside cached responses to power semantic lookups but the full pipeline isn't complete yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Embedding-Cache Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You store embedding vectors alongside cached LLM responses so semantically similar queries are served from cache without hitting the model.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Computer-Vision Cloud Consumer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You deploy CV models through cloud managed endpoints without containerising or running your own inference stack.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Managed Endpoint CV Specialist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You deploy computer-vision models exclusively through cloud managed endpoints, keeping infrastructure overhead minimal.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The GPU-Cluster CV Deployer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You deploy CV inference containers to GPU clusters but haven't yet containerised the full stack for production serving.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Containerised GPU Inference Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You containerise CV inference code and deploy it to GPU-backed cloud instances, running your own production inference stack.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Mobile Vision Model Compressor
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You distil large vision models to fit mobile devices but haven't applied post-training quantisation to hit memory targets yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Post-Training Quantisation Practitioner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You apply post-training quantisation to shrink vision models to fit edge-device memory constraints without full retraining.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Weight-Quantisation Researcher
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You quantise vision model weights after training to shrink model size and speed up inference on edge hardware.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Edge Quantisation Specialist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You quantise vision model weights after training to reduce size and accelerate inference on memory-constrained edge hardware.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Synthetic Data Strategist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use generative models to build synthetic training data but haven't applied domain randomisation techniques yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Domain Randomisation Practitioner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You use domain randomisation to generate varied synthetic images that improve your classifier's generalisation across environments.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Diffusion-Data Augmenter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You generate synthetic training images with diffusion models to fill labelling gaps, but the full pipeline isn't closed yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Diffusion-Powered Training Set Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You generate synthetic training images with diffusion models to fill gaps where real labelled data is scarce.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Domain-Randomisation Augmenter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You randomise training images across environments for generalisation but haven't fine-tuned diffusion models on domain data yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Randomisation-Plus-Diffusion Researcher
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You combine domain randomisation augmentation with diffusion model fine-tuning to build richer computer-vision training sets.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Domain-Specific Diffusion Fine-Tuner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You fine-tune diffusion models on domain images but haven't yet used outputs to generate downstream classification training data.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Diffusion-to-Classifier Pipeline Owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You fine-tune diffusion models on domain-specific images to generate training data that directly improves downstream classification.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Offline-First Edge AI Developer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You deploy CV models to edge IoT devices running inference entirely without a cloud connection during operation.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Embedded IoT CV Practitioner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run computer-vision inference on embedded IoT devices with no cloud dependency during inference.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The ONNX Edge Exporter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You export vision models to ONNX for hardware-accelerated inference on embedded processors but haven't fully standardised the workflow.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Hardware-Accelerated Edge Inference Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You export vision models to ONNX to run hardware-accelerated inference directly on embedded edge processors.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The ONNX-TensorRT Compiler
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You compile edge vision models to ONNX or TensorRT for hardware acceleration but haven't added latency profiling before each update.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Hardware-Accelerated Edge Vision Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You compile edge vision models to ONNX or TensorRT to fully exploit hardware acceleration on target devices.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Frame-Latency-Profiling Edge Developer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You profile frame-level latency and memory on edge hardware before shipping but haven't formalised full benchmark runs yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Edge-Hardware Benchmark Gatekeeper
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You benchmark frame-level latency and peak memory on target edge hardware before releasing any vision model update.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Memory-Budget Vision Optimizer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You quantise or prune models for edge devices but haven't set up per-layer sparsity validation to catch accuracy regressions.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Footprint-Trimmed Edge AI Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You quantise or prune vision models to fit within the memory and compute budgets of your edge devices.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Pruning-Sparsity Validator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You validate per-layer sparsity statistics after pruning to catch accuracy regressions before promoting a model update.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Sparsity-Threshold Enforcer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You validate per-layer sparsity statistics after every pruning run, ensuring accuracy never regresses beyond your acceptance threshold.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The CUDA Kernel Performance Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You write custom CUDA kernels to squeeze inference latency beyond standard library limits on edge and cloud hardware.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Kernel-Fusion Inference Specialist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You write custom CUDA kernels to fuse operations and reduce memory bandwidth on performance-critical inference paths.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The GPU Memory Bandwidth Optimizer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You author custom CUDA kernels to fuse layers and minimise memory transfers, without a formalised kernel development workflow yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Custom-Kernel GPU Architect
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You author custom CUDA kernels that fuse layers and minimise memory transfers, squeezing every available cycle out of GPU inference.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Creative AI Explorer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You work with image and video AI across a range of approaches without settling on one specialised workflow yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Cloud Image API Integrator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You generate images through cloud APIs like DALL-E or Midjourney, keeping your stack free of local model infrastructure.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Runtime Image Embedder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You call an image-generation API at runtime but don't yet inject the returned image directly into your product UI on the fly.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The On-the-Fly Image UI Integrator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You call an image-generation API at runtime and embed the returned image directly into your product UI on the fly.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The API-Driven Creative Platform Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You trigger image generation through an API and embed results in product UIs, without yet injecting dynamic brand parameters.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Brand-Parameterised Image Caller
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You parameterise image-generation API requests to inject dynamic brand colours, logos, or layouts at call time.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Dynamic Asset Injection Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You parameterise API requests with brand assets but haven't yet fully personalised outputs per product context.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Per-Context Brand Asset Injector
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You inject dynamic brand assets into image-generation API requests so outputs are personalised to each product context.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Self-Hosted GPU Image Generator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You batch-generate images on a self-hosted GPU, bypassing per-image cloud API costs at volume.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Bulk-Schedule Image Job Runner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You schedule bulk image-generation jobs on a self-hosted GPU to avoid per-call cloud costs at scale.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The GPU Batch-Generation Optimizer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You batch image-generation requests on a self-hosted server to cut per-image costs compared to cloud API pricing.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The High-Volume GPU Image Producer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You batch image-generation on a self-hosted GPU server, keeping per-image costs well below cloud API pricing at scale.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Self-Hosted Stable Diffusion Operator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run Stable Diffusion on a self-hosted GPU but haven't yet containerised or orchestrated the stack on a GPU node pool.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Zero-Cloud-Fee Diffusion Runner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run Stable Diffusion on a self-hosted GPU to eliminate cloud per-image fees when generating images at high volume.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Containerised Diffusion Stack Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You containerise your Stable Diffusion setup and point it at a GPU node pool without fully orchestrating it for high-volume generation yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The GPU Node Pool Diffusion Orchestrator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You containerise your Stable Diffusion stack and orchestrate it on a dedicated GPU node pool for high-volume generation.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The LoRA-and-ControlNet Explorer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You know LoRA and ControlNet but use plain text prompts rather than fully committing to either conditioning technique.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Prompt-First Image Creator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You steer image generation purely with text prompts, skipping LoRA adapters and ControlNet conditioning for simplicity.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The LoRA Aesthetic Trainer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You train LoRA adapters on proprietary style examples but haven't locked the base model into reproducing a specific aesthetic yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Style-Locking LoRA Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You train LoRA adapters on proprietary style examples to make the diffusion model consistently reproduce a specific brand aesthetic.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The ControlNet Brand Specialist
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You fine-tune ControlNet adapters on brand-specific images to lock down visual structure across generated outputs.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The ControlNet Structure Enforcer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You fine-tune ControlNet adapters on brand-specific image sets to enforce consistent visual structure across generated outputs.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The In-House ControlNet Trainer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You train ControlNet adapters on in-house image collections to enforce compositional and structural consistency in your outputs.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Structural Consistency ControlNet Expert
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You train ControlNet adapters on in-house image collections to enforce compositional and structural consistency across all generated outputs.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Automated Brand Kit Pipeline Owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run automated diffusion pipelines that accept brand kits as input but haven't wired them to a DAM or CMS yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Brand-Kit to Asset Factory Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run automated diffusion pipelines that accept a brand kit as input and emit production-ready creative assets end-to-end.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Diffusion-CMS Integrator
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You wire automated diffusion pipelines to a CMS so generated assets can publish without manual downloads or uploads.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Auto-Publish Generative Asset Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You integrate your image-generation pipeline with a CMS so that approved generated assets publish automatically without manual steps.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The DAM-Connected Generative Pipeline Owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You publish generated assets to a DAM with structured metadata, enabling creative teams to discover and reuse them.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Metadata-Tagged Asset Publisher
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You publish generated assets to a digital asset management system with structured metadata so creative teams can discover them.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Structured Metadata DAM Builder
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You tag generated assets with structured metadata and upload them to a DAM so downstream teams can filter and reuse them.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.headline: The Full Generative Asset Operations Engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:no|q_d.description: You run end-to-end pipelines that generate, tag with structured metadata, and publish assets to a DAM for team-wide reuse.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: RAG builder still finding your footing
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You wire up retrieval-augmented pipelines with LangChain and Pinecone, but haven't yet locked in a go-to orchestration pattern. The foundations are solid; the opinions are still forming.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: LangChain orchestrator, raw-prompt skeptic
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You reach for LangChain to string together multi-step retrieval chains rather than crafting prompts by hand. Semantic Kernel and Pinecone round out your go-to stack.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: RAG tuner without the LangChain deep-dive
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You adjust LangChain retrieval settings to cut hallucinations, but stop short of obsessing over fetch-k and score thresholds. Good instincts, room to go deeper.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Production RAG tuner, hallucination hunter
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You dial fetch-k and score thresholds in LangChain until your pipeline stops making things up. Keeping answers grounded is the obsession; prompt engineering is just a side hobby.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Hybrid search explorer, weight-tuning agnostic
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You combine BM25 and dense vectors to boost recall, but aren't yet blending their weights systematically. You know hybrid search helps; you're still figuring out by how much.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Hybrid search practitioner with recall ambitions
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You combine BM25 keyword retrieval with dense vector search to improve overall recall in your RAG pipelines. When sparse and dense agree, you trust the answer a little more.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Hybrid retrieval engineer, not yet weight-obsessed
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You blend sparse and dense signals for better recall, but leave the relative weighting to defaults. The combination is live; the fine-tuning is next on the list.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Hybrid-search architect, weighting as a discipline
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You tune the relative weights of BM25 sparse retrieval and dense vector search to squeeze out every recall point. Most engineers pick one; you make both work together.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cross-encoder curious, eval metrics still pending
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You re-rank with cross-encoders but MRR and nDCG against labelled sets isn't routine yet. The metrics exist; you haven't operationalized them.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Retrieval quality measurer with labelled eval sets
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You track MRR and nDCG against a human-labelled benchmark to know whether your retrieval is actually improving. Gut feel is not enough; you need the number.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Retrieval improver without the longitudinal dashboard
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You measure retrieval quality with MRR and nDCG but don't track trends over time against a persistent eval dataset. You know today's score; history is next.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Longitudinal retrieval quality tracker
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You maintain a labelled eval dataset and use MRR or nDCG to track whether each change actually moves the needle. Retrieval quality is a number you own, not a feeling.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cross-encoder re-ranker, still sharpening the edge
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You use cross-encoders to re-rank top retrieval candidates, but haven't nailed a consistent rescoring workflow for every pipeline. The signal is there; the process isn't locked in yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cross-encoder champion, passages ranked before generation
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You rescore the top retrieved passages with a cross-encoder model before anything reaches the LLM. Bi-encoder recall is just the first round; cross-encoder precision is what counts.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Systematic cross-encoder practitioner, almost all the way
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run a cross-encoder rescoring step on most retrieval result sets, but it's not yet applied universally before every generation phase. Consistent, not yet complete.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cross-encoder devotee, no retrieval passes unscored
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Every retrieval result set goes through a cross-encoder before the LLM ever sees it. You treat re-ranking as a mandatory pipeline step, not an optional quality improvement.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Embedding-first builder, drift still an open question
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Embeddings are central to how you build, but monitoring for model drift and automated re-indexing aren't on your radar yet. The index is live; staleness is a future problem.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Embedding drift watcher, re-index on shift
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You track cosine similarity distributions to catch embedding drift, and trigger re-indexing when score patterns shift. Your index reflects today's data, not last quarter's.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cosine-distribution observer, snapshot strategy forming
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You track embedding model drift by comparing cosine similarity distributions over time, but the periodic snapshot cadence isn't fully automated yet. The signal exists; the schedule is next.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Periodic drift spotter, index freshness guaranteed
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You compare cosine similarity distributions across periodic index snapshots and re-index when drift is detected. Your embedding model's age doesn't catch you by surprise.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Automated re-indexer, nightly pipeline not quite there
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You trigger incremental re-indexing when source documents update, but a fully automated nightly pipeline isn't running yet. The trigger exists; the schedule is still manual.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Nightly re-indexer, no manual intervention needed
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: New documents enter your vector store automatically via nightly re-indexing pipelines. Manual index updates are something you remember doing once, before you automated it.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Automated indexer, propagation not yet zero-touch
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You automate nightly re-indexing for new documents, but source document propagation still has a manual step before it reaches the vector store. Almost fully hands-off, not quite.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Zero-touch nightly re-indexing engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Updated source documents propagate to your vector store every night, hands-free. If a document changes, the index reflects it before morning.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Embedding drift monitor, combined evals not yet live
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You monitor embedding drift to schedule re-indexing, but offline evals that combine retrieval recall and generation quality into one score aren't part of your workflow yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: End-to-end pipeline quality scorer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run offline evaluations that produce a single combined score covering both retrieval recall and generation quality. One metric tells you whether the whole pipeline is healthy.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Pipeline health scorer, single metric not yet unified
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run offline evals measuring retrieval recall and generation quality, but haven't combined them into a single pipeline-health score yet. Two good numbers, still waiting on the rollup.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Unified pipeline health metric owner
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You combine retrieval recall and generation quality scores into a single pipeline health metric you can trend over time. When that number drops, you know exactly which layer to inspect.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Weekly eval runner, CI gates not yet blocking
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run weekly automated evals of retrieval precision and recall against a golden set, but retrieval quality dashboards aren't yet wired into CI to block bad merges.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Retrieval quality CI gatekeeper
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You connect retrieval quality dashboards to your CI pipeline so regressions block merges before they reach users. Bad retrieval can't ship; the pipeline says so.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: CI-gated retrieval engineer, full enforcement pending
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You integrate quality dashboards into CI to catch regressions, but the strictest gate-all-or-nothing enforcement isn't fully locked down yet. Almost there.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Retrieval regression blocker, zero tolerance policy
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: CI quality dashboards catch every retrieval regression before it merges. If the recall score drops, the PR doesn't ship. That's the policy, and you maintain it.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Self-hosted vector store builder, Weaviate-Postgres split
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run your own vector store rather than paying for managed infrastructure, but Weaviate and PostgreSQL live on separate deployments. You own the hardware; the architecture is still evolving.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Weaviate-Postgres shared deployment engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run Weaviate alongside PostgreSQL in one self-hosted deployment so vector and relational queries share infrastructure. One less service boundary at query time.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Weaviate-Postgres colocation starter, same-host not yet
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run Weaviate alongside PostgreSQL in a shared deployment, but having both on exactly the same host isn't quite configured yet. Close, but not co-resident.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Same-host vector and relational query engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Weaviate and PostgreSQL share a single host so vector similarity and relational queries run without a network hop between them. Simplicity and control in one deployment.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: HNSW tuner, QPS target still aspirational
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You tune Weaviate's HNSW parameters to balance recall against query speed, but haven't locked in ef_construction and m values against a specific QPS target yet. Tuning in progress.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Weaviate HNSW QPS optimizer

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You maintain a documented AI threat model that lists prompt-injection and data-leakage attack vectors. Knowing what can go wrong is the first step to preventing it.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You document prompt-injection and data-leakage attack vectors, but the threat model isn't yet kept as a living document that tracks data-exfiltration surfaces specifically. Almost a living record.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Living AI threat model owner, exfil surfaces tracked
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You maintain a living AI threat model that documents prompt-injection and data-exfiltration attack surfaces as they evolve. The threat model grows with the system.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Cross-functional AI threat model sharer, bias audits next
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You share AI threat models in cross-functional security reviews, but demographic bias audits before customer-facing releases aren't in place yet. Safety sharing is live; bias testing is next.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Demographic bias auditor before every release
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run bias audits against demographic benchmarks before any customer-facing AI feature ships. Responsible AI is a pre-launch checklist item, not a retrospective exercise.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Pre-ship bias auditor, per-feature universality forming
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You conduct demographic bias audits on customer-facing AI features before they ship, but universal application to every feature isn't consistent yet. Most features pass through audit; all should.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Mandatory demographic bias audit enforcer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Every customer-facing AI feature goes through a demographic bias audit before it ships to production. Bias testing is not optional, and the process doesn't have exceptions.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Living threat model owner, automated red-teaming next
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You maintain a living threat model that tracks new attack surfaces as your AI system evolves, but automated red-team prompt suites that block deployment on failure aren't running yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Automated red-team safety gate engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run automated red-team prompt suites against model releases and block deployment when any safety test fails. Safety failures are bugs; bugs don't ship.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Automated red-teamer, per-release blocking still tightening
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You run automated red-team suites and block on failures, but applying the gate consistently to every release isn't complete yet. The automation is real; coverage is expanding.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Zero-tolerance red-team deployment blocker
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: Automated red-team prompt suites run against every model release, and any safety-test failure blocks deployment. Safety is not negotiated at the last minute; it's tested on every build.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Red-team automator, external audit reporting pending
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You automate red-team prompts against each model release and block on failures, but publishing safety evaluation results to regulators and external auditors on a fixed schedule isn't in place yet.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: External AI safety report publisher
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You publish AI safety evaluation results to regulators and external auditors on a fixed reporting schedule. Accountability is built into the calendar, not just the culture.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Scheduled safety reporter, fixed-schedule framing forming
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You publish AI safety evaluation results to regulators and auditors, but the fixed-schedule framing for external reporting isn't fully formalized yet. The reports go out; the cadence is solidifying.

q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.headline: Regulatory-grade AI safety compliance engineer
q1_domain:data|q_d_root:yes|q_d_y:yes|q_.description: You provide AI safety evaluation reports to external regulators and auditors on a fixed schedule, every time. Compliance is not a one-off audit; it's a recurring operational commitment.

q1_domain:specialty|q_s_root:no|q_s_n:ye.description: Your core product runs entirely on no-code tools but paying users haven't yet validated the model at real scale.

q1_domain:specialty|q_s_root:no|q_s_n:ye.description: Your fully no-code product has paying customers, growing without a single line of backend code written.

q1_domain:specialty|q_s_root:no|q_s_n:ye.headline: The No-Code Founder Who Proved the Model

q1_domain:specialty|q_s_root:no|q_s_n:ye.description: You contribute code to your startup every single day and rarely take time off from shipping — rest is optional.

q1_domain:specialty|q_s_root:no|q_s_n:ye.description: You set a daily commit streak target and hit it consistently — your startup gets code every single day without fail.
